description: Fieldlink integration protocol. Defines how Polyhedral-Intelligence connects to the ecosystem: what it exports, what it consumes, and the handshake format.

exports.description: Data and interfaces Polyhedral-Intelligence provides to other repos.

endpoints.description: Master schema: 20 Families (icosahedron) with full equations, 12 Principles (dodecahedron), metadata. The richest structured data source in the ecosystem.
Lightweight family/principle reference without equation weight.
Maps 20 Families to 5 meta-fields: Chemical (F01-F04), Emotional (F05-F08), Cognitive (F09-F11), Dream (F12-F17), Symbolic (F18-F20).
Index of all atlas entries with IDs and titles.
Atlas entries — seed glyphs, family/principle sweeps, noise-to-insight reframes, refined glyphs.
Master catalog of all family and principle equation files.

imports.description: Data Polyhedral-Intelligence can consume from ecosystem repos.

sources.use: Shape-to-meaning translation for the two core polyhedra. Bridge mappings linking families/principles to sensors, defenses, and protocols.
Octahedral computation model — 8 states, fractal ring schema, energy relaxation. Bridges mandala protocol steps to computational ground states.
Agent lifecycle logic — seed compression, amplitude vectors (6 channels: structure, flow, connection, autonomy, transcendence, grounding), elder archive pattern.
FELT field-event detection, emotion-glyph bindings for integrated.py bridge classes.
Extended glyph registry (85+ entries), animal glyphs, recovered glyphs, Principles Lattice with emoji symbols.

handshake.description: Protocol for establishing a fieldlink connection.
steps: 1. Consumer reads protocols/connect.json to discover exports.
2. Consumer fetches the exported JSON files listed in exports.endpoints.
4. Consumer maps imported data to its internal representation.
5. Both repos record the link in their .fieldlink.json metadata.
handshake.schema_versioning: semver — major bump = breaking change, minor = additive, patch = fix
handshake.discovery: Repos can be discovered via PROJECTS.md or .fieldlink.json sources list.
